CREATE TABLE test_urls (id INT PRIMARY KEY, url CLOB);
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (1, 'https://example.com/path?query=1#fragment');
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (2, 'ftp://user:pass@ftp.example.com');
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (3, 'http://localhost:8000/page');
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (4, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (5, 'mailto:someone@example.com');
INSERT INTO test_urls VALUES (6, 'https://user@subdomain.example.org');
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 1;
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 2;
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 3;
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 4;
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 5;
SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 6;
SELECT (SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 1) AS user1, (SELECT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE id = 6) AS user2;
SELECT id, sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE url IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_urls WHERE sys.getuser(url) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT sys.getuser(url) FROM test_urls WHERE url LIKE 'https://%';
CREATE TABLE additional_data (url_id INT, description CLOB, FOREIGN KEY(url_id) REFERENCES test_urls(id));
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (1, 'A secure link to example.com');
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (2, 'An FTP link with credentials');
SELECT ad.description, sys.getuser(t.url) FROM additional_data ad JOIN test_urls t ON ad.url_id = t.id WHERE t.url IS NOT NULL;
